title: Federal Law No. 12:
text:
Federal
Law No. 12

Issued
on 27/11/2023

Corresponding
to 13/05/1445 H.

ON
THE REGULATION OF THE PARTNERSHIP BETWEEN THE PUBLIC AND PRIVATE
SECTORS 




We,
Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the United Arab Emirates,

-
After perusal of the Constitution; 

-
Federal Law No. (1) of 1972 on the Competencies of Ministries and the Powers of
Ministers, and

its
amendments;

-
Federal Law No. (5) of 1985 On the Promulgation of the Civil Transactions Law,
and its

amendments;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (15) of 2018 on the Collection of Revenues and Public
Funds; 

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (16) of 2018 On the Federal Government Real Estate
Property;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (26) of 2019 On the Public Finance, and its
amendments;

-
And Federal-Decree Law No. (32) of 2021 On Commercial Companies; 

-
And based on the proposal of the Minister of Finance, the approval of the
Council of Ministers

and
the Federal National Council and the ratification of the Federal Supreme
Council;

Have
issued the following Law:

Article
1 - Definitions 

In
application of the provisions of this Law, the following terms and expressions
shall have the meanings assigned against each, unless the context requires
otherwise:

UAE:
The United Arab Emirates.

Government:
The Federal Government of the UAE. 

Ministry:
The Ministry of Finance.

Minister:
The Minister of Finance.

Federal
Public Sector: Any ministry established in accordance with Federal Law No. (1)
of 1972 On the Competencies of Ministries and the Powers of Ministers, and its
amendments, as well as any federal authority, institution or regulatory bodies 
or any other entities affiliated with the Government.

Relevant
Federal Entity: The federal entity in charge of tendering the project. 

General
Budget Committee: The committee formed by the Council of Ministers.

Private
Sector: The companies and business enterprises (excluding the companies and
business enterprises that are wholly owned by the Government).

Potential
Partnership Project: Any project which - upon evaluation of its feasibility-
proves that it meets the Partnership Project criteria outlined in the
Partnership Projects Guidebook.

Partnership
Project: A contractual relationship bringing together a federal entity (or
several federal entities) and one or more Partners from the Private Sector, that
is established in accordance with this Law for the provision of a public service
or the operation of a public facility.

Partner:
Any legal person or consortium of legal persons from the Private Sector that is
a party to the Partnership Contract.

Project
Company: Any company or firm established by the Partner to implement the
project.

Project
Agreement: A contract concluded by the Relevant Federal Entity with the Partner
to regulate the relationship between the parties regarding the Partnership
Project.

Project
Team: The team in charge of developing the project idea and tendering the
project.

Grievance
Committee: A grievance committee formed by the Minister in accordance with the
provisions of Clause (2) of Article (20) of this Law.

Partnership
Projects Guidebook: A guide issued in accordance with the provisions of this
Law, that contains the rules, provisions and procedures related to Partnership
Projects.

Article
2 - Objectives of the Law 

The
Law shall aim to achieve the following objectives: 

1-
Regulate Partnership Projects between the Federal Public Sector and the Private
Sector in the UAE.

2-
Encourage the Private Sector to participate in development and strategic
projects, and boost investment in projects of economic, social and service
value.

3-
Enable the Government to implement strategic projects in an efficient and
effective manner.

4-
Utilise the financial, administrative, regulatory, technical and technological
potential and expertise available in the Private Sector, thus enabling the
community to avail of the best services at the best value for money.

5-
Increase productivity and improve the quality of public services while ensuring
efficient management practices to develop those services.

6-
Transfer knowledge and expertise from the Private Sector to the federal
entities, and train and qualify the employees of the federal entities in the UAE
to manage and operate the projects.

7-
Implement projects that add value to the public funds. 

8-
Reduce the financial and operational risks that may be incurred by the
Government from the implementation of the projects.

9-
Bring a shift in the management of certain infrastructure and public service
projects from direct implementation, operation and management to other forms of
implementation according to approved policies and pursuant to controls for
ensuring quality.

10-
Boost the competitiveness of projects on local, regional and international
markets.

Article
3 - Scope of Application 

The
provisions of this Law shall apply to any Partnership Project that is wholly or
partially funded by the Private Sector and tendered by a federal entity.

Article
4 - Exemptions 

The
provisions of this Law shall not apply to:

1-
The partnership contracts concluded prior to the entry into force of this Law,
in a manner not inconsistent with the provisions of Article (32) of this Law.


2-
The outsourcing of the services specified in the Partnership Projects
Guidebook.

3-
The projects whose value is less than the financial threshold stipulated in the
Partnership Projects Guidebook.

4-
The projects for the privatisation of public assets and services. 

5-
The supply and procurement contracts related to national security that are
specified in the Partnership Projects Guidebook.

6-
The federal entities, sectors and projects that are excluded pursuant to the
Cabinet decision.

Article
5 - Competencies of the Ministry 

For
the purposes of implementing this Law, the Ministry shall exercise the following
competencies:

1-
Propose the Partnership Projects Guidebook and submit it to the Council of
Ministers for approval.

2-
Prepare and issue a guide for the management and implementation of the
Partnership Project, including the provisions regulating the management and
oversight of Partnership Projects during the implementation phase.

3-
Prepare, issue and amend the guides and all forms and documents related to the
Partnership Projects, subject to Clause (1) of this Article. 

4-
Propose to update the legislation regulating the Partnership Projects between
the federal entities and the Private Sector in accordance with the best
international practices.

5-
Provide the appropriate environment to attract the Private Sector to participate
in Partnership Projects and overcome difficulties that may hinder the success of
Partnership Projects. 

6-
Provide assistance and support to the federal entities in the process of
tendering Partnership Projects in accordance with this Law if need be, and
encourage the federal entities to make use of the Partnership Projects
method.

7-
Examine and evaluate the offers and feasibility studies submitted by the federal
entities and the Private Sector and determine the feasibility of tendering them
as a Partnership Project, after coordination with the relevant entities if need
be.

8-
Direct the federal entities regarding the mechanism for preparing the
feasibility studies and other studies on the evaluation of Partnership
Projects.

9-
Offer advice to the federal entities regarding the tendering and implementation
of Partnership Projects, if need be, at the Ministry’s discretion.

10-
Create a data record for Partnership Projects.

11-
Gauge the success of Partnership Projects on a periodic basis and submit
periodic reports thereon to the General Budget Committee. 

Article
6 - Competencies of the Relevant Federal Entity

For
the purposes of implementing this Law, the Relevant Federal Entity shall
exercise the following competencies:

1-
Propose Partnership Projects and prepare all necessary studies to evaluate the
Partnership Project and determine the financial, economic, technical and social
feasibility of the project and the risks and implications thereof in accordance
with the requirements of the Partnership Projects Guidebook. 

2-
Prepare the initial budget for the Partnership Project in cooperation with the
Project Team in accordance with the budget preparation guides and forms issued
by the Ministry and the Partnership Projects Guidebook.

3-
Determine all technical specifications and requirements related to the
Partnership Project. 

4-
Coordinate with the Ministry to formulate the structure of the Partnership
Project, including the specification of the Partnership methods that are
suitable for the nature of the project and the nature of contribution of both
parties to the Partnership in the project as well as the distribution of shares
and financial returns resulting therefrom.

5-
Propose the fees for the services related to the Partnership Project.

6-
Support the Project Team in the preparation of the tender documents and the
Terms of Reference for the Partnership Project. 

7-
Implement its obligations stipulated in the Project Agreement and exercise the
rights contained therein.

8-
Coordinate with the other government and federal entities concerned with the
project to ensure that the outputs of the Partnership Project are aligned with
their plans. 

9-
Oversee the proper implementation of the Partnership Projects by the Private
Sector in accordance with the provisions of Article (25) of this Law, without
prejudice to the competencies of the other regulatory bodies. 

10-
Overcome the difficulties that prevent Partnership Projects from achieving their
intended objectives in coordination with the competent authorities in the UAE.


11-
Prepare periodic quarterly reports for each Partnership Project it tenders and
submit them to the Ministry.

Article
7- Formation of the Project Team 

1-
A Project Team shall be formed for each Potential Partnership Project. The
Partnership Projects Guidebook shall determine the mechanism for forming the
team.

2-
The Project Team shall undertake the following:

a-
Coordinate and manage the Partnership Project tender procedures and the
selection of the Partner. 

b-
Prepare the initial budget for the project in coordination with the Relevant
Federal Entity.

c-
Prepare the tender documents and the Terms of Reference for the Partnership
Project as well as the Project Agreement. 

d-
Evaluate and open the offers pursuant to the evaluation procedures and criteria
specified in the tender documents and the Partnership Projects Guidebook.

e-
Ensure the application of the principles of transparency, fair competition and
equal opportunities in accordance with the provisions of this Law and the
Partnership Projects Guidebook. 

f-
Any other competencies stipulated in this Law or in the Partnership Projects
Guidebook.

Article
8 - Project Selection 

1-
The Relevant Federal Entity, Ministry or Private Sector may propose the
Partnership Project. 

2-
The Ministry shall decide with the Relevant Federal Entity whether the project
that is proposed to be tendered as a Partnership Project merits selection as a
Potential Partnership Project, in accordance with the criteria set out in the
Partnership Projects Guidebook.

3-
When a project is selected as a Potential Partnership Project, it shall be
evaluated pursuant to the procedures stipulated in the Partnership Projects
Guidebook.

4-
The Partnership Projects Guidebook shall specify the criteria and studies
required in the project selection and approval stages. 

Article
9 - Project Proposal by the Federal Entity 

The
federal entity that wishes to contract for a Partnership Project shall be under
the following obligations:

1-
Ensure that the project meets the Partnership Project criteria set out in this
Law and the Partnership Projects Guidebook, and examine the adequacy of
tendering any of its projects as a Partnership Project. 

2-
Tender the project using an alternative method where there are strategic reasons
or reasons of public interest.

3-
Submit proof that it has considered the Partnership Project option for its
projects whenever the Ministry requests it to do so.

4-
Prepare a project demonstration whenever it wishes to contract a Partnership
Project, including justifications for the project selection, and submit it to
the Ministry pursuant to the procedures set out in the Partnership Projects
Guidebook.



Article
10 - Project Proposal by the Private Sector 

1-
The Private Sector may present an initiative to any federal entity or to the
Ministry to tender a project as a Partnership Project. The federal entity shall,
whenever presented directly with such initiative, submit it to the Ministry
pursuant to the procedures set out in this Law and the Partnership Projects
Guidebook.

2-
The Partnership Projects Guidebook shall outline the provisions that regulate
the Partnership Projects initiatives presented by the Private Sector, including
the following provisions:

a-
The mechanism for presenting initiatives and the method of evaluating, accepting
and tendering them. 

b-
The guarantees for protecting confidential information, intellectual property
rights, trade secrets and any special exclusive rights associated with the
initiative.

c-
The incentives and benefits offered to the author of the initiative. 

Article
11 - Approval of the Project 

1-
The federal entity or the Private Sector shall submit the project to the
Ministry for approval as a Potential Partnership Project, including the details
set out in the Partnership Projects Guidebook.

2-
The Ministry shall examine and evaluate the project in accordance with the
requirements of this Law and the Partnership Projects Guidebook.

3-
If the project meets the Partnership Projects requirements and there is no
financial allocation therefor, the Ministry shall submit the offer for initial
approval of the project by the General Budget Committee and upon its approval,
the project shall be submitted to the Council of Ministers for final
approval.

4-
The Ministry may submit the project to the Council of Ministers in case of
financial allocation therefor.

5-
If the Council of Ministers approves the project, the Ministry shall notify the
Relevant Federal Entity of the approval of the project.

6-
The Partnership Projects Guidebook shall determine the approvals required at
each of the stages of development and tendering of the Partnership
Project.

Article
12 - Approval of the Budget

1-
The Project Team shall, in cooperation with the Relevant Federal Entity, develop
the initial budget for the Partnership Project in accordance with the budget
preparation provisions issued by the Ministry. 

2-
It shall not be permissible to tender any Partnership Project that incurs costs
on any federal entity unless the cost of the project for the duration of its
implementation is fully approved by the Government.

Article
13 - Government Financial Guarantees

1-
Subject to the legislation in force in the UAE, the Relevant Federal Entity may
- based on the project needs - propose to the Ministry to issue a government
guarantee to cover the financial obligations of the federal entity stipulated in
the Project Agreement clauses.

2-
The Partnership Projects Guidebook shall set out the conditions and procedures
for requesting a government financial guarantee, and the mechanisms for deciding
thereon.

Article
14 - Types of Partnership Projects 

Partnership
Projects that are effective hereunder shall be of one of the following
types:

1-
Build-Operate-Transfer.

2-
Build-Own-Operate-Transfer.

3-
Build-Own-Operate.

4-
Financial benefit from assets.

5-
Build-Own-Lease-Transfer.

6-
Management contracts.

7-
Any other types specified in the Partnership Projects Guidebook. 

Article
15 - Tendering Methods and Procedures 

1-
Partnership Projects shall be tendered using one of the following methods:

a-
Two-stage method: Whereby the tendering shall be as follows:

1.
First stage: During which a pre-qualification is conducted for a group of
Private Sector entities that have expressed interest in the project.

2.
Second stage: During which the complete tender documents are issued exclusively
to the potential Partners who have qualified.

b-
Emergency procedure method: The emergency procedure method may be used for
tendering, whereby it consists of a single stage without a qualification
procedure, in any of the following cases:

1.
Where there is a need to tender the project urgently.

2.
If the Project Team deems that there is no need for the qualification procedure
in the presence of a limited number of suitable potential Partners on the market
or in the absence of a complex technical aspect of the project, which makes the
competition limited to the financial aspects only, or for any other reason that
does not require the qualification of the Partners.

c-
Direct appointment method: The direct appointment method may be used for
tendering, whereas the Relevant Federal Entity negotiates directly with one or
more potential Partners. This method shall only be used in the following
cases:

1.
Where there is a need to provide assets or provide a service urgently and it is
not suitable to use the procedures set out in Paragraphs (a) and (b) of Clause
(1) of this Article.

2.
Where there is a single potential Partner on the market with the ability to
implement the project.

3.
Where all or some of the project components are associated with or related to
national security or the protection of State secrets.

4.
Any other necessary and urgent matters related to the public interest, provided
that the Minister approves the use of this procedure for tendering them.

2-
In the event that there is a single potential Partner for the project, the
Relevant Federal Entity may terminate the bid or proceed with the tender and
award the project to the potential Partner, subject to the following two
conditions:

a-
Fulfilment of the tender documents requirements.

b-
Evidence of adequate value for money in the offer for the value-for-money
assessment.

3-
The Partnership Projects Guidebook shall determine the procedures for each of
the tendering methods set out in this Article. 

Article
16 - Submission of Offers

1-
The offer for the Partnership Project shall be submitted either by one Partner
from the Private Sector or by a consortium of several Partners from the Private
Sector.

2-
In case the offer is made by a consortium of Partners, it shall be submitted in
the name of the consortium, and none of the members of the consortium may submit
individual offers whether directly or through another consortium, or may submit
the offer by a company where one of the consortium members owns the majority of
its capital or has control over its management, unless otherwise stipulated in
the terms of the tender or this is done with the approval of the Relevant
Federal Entity.

3-
Any federal entity may participate in the tendering of the Partnership Project,
upon approval of its participation by the Ministry.

Article
17 - Evaluation of Offers 

1-
The Project Team shall examine and evaluate the offers submitted from the
technical, financial and legal aspects, and shall determine the accepted offers
and those that are eliminated, provided that each offer is awarded an evaluation
score in accordance with the evaluation criteria set out in the tender
documents.

2-
The Project Team shall submit its recommendations on the results and evaluation
of the offers, including a list of the offers that have achieved the minimum
evaluation threshold to the Ministry and the Relevant Federal Entity for
approval.

3-
The Project Team may decide to request additional clarifications after
submission of the offers, including but not limited to requesting the bidders to
submit their “best and final offer”, whenever it deems this action
to be in the interest of the project, and in coordination with the Ministry and
the Relevant Federal Entity. 

4-
If the Project Team fails to agree on the terms of the project and the Project
Agreement with the best bidder according to the evaluation results, the Project
Team shall have the right to halt the discussion with the best bidder and move
on to negotiate the terms of the project and the Project Agreement with the
following bidders by descending order on the list of offers with the best
evaluation.

Article
18 - Cancellation or Modification of the Tender Procedures or the Project 

1-
The Relevant Federal Entity may, in coordination with the Ministry, cancel or
modify the tender procedures or the project at any time before awarding the
project.

2-
No bidder for the Partnership Project shall have the right to claim compensation
for the cancellation or modification of the tender or the project.

Article
19 - Announcement of the Tender Award 

The
Relevant Federal Entity shall notify the successful bidder that the bid has been
awarded to him, and it shall inform the remaining bidders of the result of the
bid award.

Article
20 - Grievance Against the Tender Procedures

1-
A Private Sector bidder for any project may submit, within a maximum of (10) ten
days from the date of the award of the tender, a grievance request to the
Ministry in the following cases:

a-
The offer documents or procedures violate the provisions of the Law, the
Partnership Projects Guidebook or the other relevant laws.

b-
The Partner selection procedures violate the provisions of the Law, the
Partnership Projects Guidebook or the other relevant laws in a manner that
affects transparency and fair competition among bidders.

c-
It is established that there was bribery, illegal commissions, bid-rigging,
fraud or abuse of power with the aim of illegally influencing the tender
procedures, to the extent that such acts have an actual impact on the results of
the tender award, without prejudice to the provision of any other relevant
law.

2-
A grievance committee shall be established by decision of the Minister to
examine the grievances filed in relation to the tender procedures. The
Partnership Projects Guidebook shall determine the grievance mechanism,
procedures and durations.

3-
The grievance request shall be based on one of the grounds referred to in Clause
(1) of this Article and it shall include a clarification of the direct impact of
those grounds on weakening the chance of winning the bid or of their being the
direct cause of his loss of the bid in the event that the bid has been awarded,
along with enclosing all the documents in support of the request.

4-
The Grievance Committee may suspend the tender procedures pending a decision on
the request, whenever it deems that the request merits consideration and that
continuing the procedures would significantly weaken the grievant’s
chances of winning the bid.

5-
If the Grievance Committee finds that the grounds for the grievance are valid,
it shall issue its recommendations to rectify the erroneous procedure, to
exclude one of the bidders, to take any other measures or to reject the
grievance for invalidity of the grounds, and it shall submit its recommendations
to the Minister for approval before taking any action.

Article
21 - Project Agreement

1-
The Project Team shall prepare the Project Agreement in coordination with the
Ministry and the Relevant Federal Entity in accordance with the provisions of
this Law and the Partnership Projects Guidebook.

2-
The Partnership Projects Guidebook shall set out the provisions to be included
in the Project Agreement, including but not limited to the provisions on the
change of the laws, a change in the economic conditions for unforeseen
circumstances at the time of contracting and the amendment of the Project
Agreement as well as the provisions on its termination and the ensuing
compensation.

3-
The Project Agreement may include a statement of the financial revenues
available to the Private Sector according to one of the following methods:

a-
Payments made for the provision of the project or the service.

b-
Granting the Partner the right to collect and retain the proceeds from the use
of an asset or a service, or to participate in the revenue resulting from such
use.

c-
Combining the two methods referred to in Paragraphs (a) and (b) of Clause (3) of
this Article.

d-
Any other method agreed upon.

4-
The Project Agreement may require the Partner to appoint national workers or
local contractors and to use local materials, equipment and goods. Any such
requirements shall be expressly disclosed in the tender documents.

5-
After the lapse of the period for filing the grievance or deciding thereupon in
accordance with Article (20) of this Law, the Project Agreement shall be signed
in Arabic - or in English if need be - by the Relevant Federal Entity and the
successful Partner.

6-
The Project Agreement shall be governed by the laws in force in the UAE.

Article
22 - Stalled Projects

1-
The Relevant Federal Entity shall replace the Partner and acquire the
Partnership Project to ensure its continued implementation whenever the Partner
fails to implement the project and such failure causes harm to the public
interest, interruption in the services of a public facility or discontinuation
of the provision of the public service of which continuation is required by the
public interest, without prejudice to the right of the federal entity to claim
any compensation for the damage resulting from the failure to implement the
project.

2-
The Partnership Projects Guidebook shall set out the provisions concerning
stalled projects.

Article
23 - Mortgage

1-
The Partner may - after obtaining the approval of the Relevant Federal Entity -
mortgage any of his assets and rights associated with the project as follows to
secure the financing he has obtained to implement the project:

a-
Mortgaging the inalienable and alienable rights of the Partner or his rights to
the assets of the Partnership Project or the Project Company.

b-
Mortgaging the right of the Partner or the Project Company, if it is owned in
the name and for the account of any of them, to the revenues and payments
resulting from the use of the facility or the service subject of the Partnership
Project.

2-
The Relevant Federal Entity shall be a party to any agreement that grants the
financing parties the right to replace the Partner in the Project Agreement, and
the Relevant Federal Entity may object to any of the financing parties.

3-
The mortgages referred to in this Article shall be governed by the provisions
regulating mortgages in accordance with the laws in force in the UAE unless the
Project Agreement stipulates otherwise.

Article
24 - Incentives and Exemptions

Incentives
may be granted to the Private Sector Partners in Partnership Projects, for the
purpose of increasing the appeal of Partnership Projects. The types of those
incentives shall be determined by a Cabinet decision based upon the
Minister’s recommendation.

Article
25 - Oversight of Project Implementation

The
Relevant Federal Entity shall have competence to oversee the project
implementation and manage the implementation phase in accordance with the
provisions of the Project Agreement and the guides and forms issued by the
Ministry. The Ministry’s role in the implementation phase shall be limited
to monitoring and providing support to the Relevant Federal Entity whenever it
so requires.

Article
26 - Content of the Partnership Projects Guidebook

The
Partnership Projects Guidebook shall set out the detailed provisions regulating
Partnership Projects, and particularly the following:

1-
The governance and procedures for tendering Partnership Projects, including the
project proposal, the value-for-money assessment, the market study, the project
structuring and management, the tender procedures, the mechanism for requesting
clarifications, the conferences and the project tendering criteria.

2-
The special requirements related to the content of the tender documents and the
Project Agreement.

3-
The special requirements related to any deadlines and time frames to be observed
regarding the tender procedures.

4-
The special requirements related to the Partner selection and offer evaluation
criteria as well as the required Project Team qualifications.

5-
The rules regulating the “best and final offer” request and the
negotiations with the potential Partner.

6-
The requirements for disclosure and publication of the basic information on the
tendering of Partnership Projects on the websites and in the media.

7-
The provisions regulating the management of the contracts and the oversight of
the Partnership Projects during the implementation phase and the procedures for
managing and implementing the Project Agreement, including the provisions for
making payments and the authority matrix determined by the federal entity.

8-
The rules regulating the change in the control, composition of Partners or
Partner structure.

9-
The rules regulating the cases of force majeure and the exceptional
circumstances that may occur in the course of the project implementation.

10-
The rules regulating the termination of the Project Agreement and the mechanism
for compensation upon termination.

11-
The rules regulating the arrangement of mortgages on the assets associated with
the project and any agreement that may grant the financing parties the right to
replace the Partner in the implementation, control or acquisition of the
project.

12-
Any other provisions that the Law allows to be regulated in the Partnership
Projects Guidebook or that the Council of Ministers decides to add.

Article
27 - Transparency and Disclosure in the Tender Procedures

1-
The tender procedures shall be subject to the principles of transparency,
freedom of competition and equal opportunities, and they shall be implemented
pursuant to the rules and procedures stipulated in this Law and the Partnership
Projects Guidebook.

2-
To achieve the principle of transparency and equal opportunities, the Relevant
Federal Entity shall make public the following information:

a-
An abstract of all the major decisions related to the approval of the
Partnership Project and the tender procedures.

b-
The decision to award the project.

Article
28 - Project Company

1-
The Partner may establish the Project Company in accordance with the laws in
force in the UAE to implement the project. The established company shall be
liable for compliance with all the clauses of the Partnership agreement, and it
may neither be replaced nor sold, its ownership may not be waived (whether
directly or indirectly) and it may not enter into a contract with a third party
without obtaining prior approval from the Relevant Federal Entity pursuant to
the same mechanism for approval of the original Partnership agreement .

2-
The foreign investor from outside the UAE may acquire the Project Company in its
entirety in accordance with the Commercial Companies Law in force in the UAE,
unless the tender documents stipulate otherwise.

Article
29 - Partnership Projects Information Centre

The
Ministry shall establish a data record for Partnership Projects and an
evaluation system for ongoing and implemented Partnership Projects. The Ministry
may, whenever it deems so appropriate, publish the information that is useful
for the public on its website.

Article
30 - Appointment of Consultants

1-
The Ministry may appoint external consultants to provide assistance in the
process of selecting, structuring, tendering, evaluating and overseeing
Partnership Projects whenever necessary.

2-
The Ministry may direct the Relevant Federal Entity or the Project Team to
appoint external consultants whenever it considers that such an action ensures
the optimal use of external resources.

Article
31 - Settlement of Disputes

The
State courts shall have jurisdiction to hear the disputes arising from the
implementation of the Project Agreement. It may be agreed to settle the disputes
through the alternative dispute resolution applicable in the UAE, including
mediation, arbitration and expert determination.

Article
32 - Amendment, Renewal or Extension of Partnership Project Agreements

1-
The Partnership Projects concluded prior to the entry into force of this Law
shall be governed by the provisions of the relevant agreements and the laws
applicable thereto prior to the issuance of this Law.

2-
Those agreements and their relevant licences may not be amended, renewed or
extended, except in accordance with the provisions of this Law, the Partnership
Projects Guidebook and the guides and forms issued by the Ministry.

Article
33 - Abrogations

1-
Any provision that contradicts or conflicts with the provisions of this Law
shall be abrogated.

2-
Cabinet Decision No. (1/1) of 2017 On the Procedures Manual for Partnership
Between Federal Entities and the Private Sector and Cabinet Decision No. (4/8)
of 2019 On the Procedures Manual for Partnership Between the Public and Private
Sectors in the UAE shall remain in force pending issuance of the Partnership
Projects Guidebook, to the extent that this does not conflict with the
provisions of this Law.

3-
The provisions of the federal legislation regulating the procurement and
warehouse management in the federal government shall not apply to the
Partnership Projects concluded in accordance with the provisions of this
Law.

Article
34 - Publication and Enforcement of the Law

This
Law shall be published in the Official Gazette and it shall come into force as
of the day following the date of its publication.

Issued
by Us at the Presidential 
Palace in Abu Dhabi:-
On: 13/05/1445
H.
Corresponding to: 27/11/2023 


Mohammed bin
Zayed Al Nahyan

President of the
United Arab Emirates


This Law was published in the Official
Gazette, Issue No. 764 (Supplement), p. 47.



[1] 
Clause (2) of Article (33) of this Law stipulated that Cabinet Decision No.
(1/1) of 2017 on the Procedures Manual for Partnership Between Federal Entities
and the Private Sector and Cabinet Decision No. (4/8) of 2019 on the Procedures
Manual for Partnership Between the Public and Private Sectors in the UAE shall
remain in force pending issuance of the Partnership Projects Guidebook, to the
extent that this does not conflict with the provisions of this Law.
Clause
(3) of the same Article stipulated that the provisions of the federal
legislation regulating the procurement and warehouse management in the federal
government shall not apply to the Partnership Projects concluded in accordance
with the provisions of this Law; thus a mention is in order.
